column1: # get / set — universal container access
# `get` and `set` are AQL's container-access pair. Both are forward-
# precedence with a fan of signatures so the same word can drive
# Stores (copy-on-write), Object instances (in-place mutation),
# Arrays (indexed by integer), and plain Map / List / Options nodes
# (read-only for `get`).
# Sig shape (see eng/go/core_storage.go):
#   set 'key' v store      copy-on-write: pushes a new layer with
#                          {'key':v}, propagates up the parent chain.
#   set field v object     in-place field mutation on an Object instance.
#   set i v array          in-place set at index i (bounds-checked).
#   get 'key' store        Store lookup; errors on missing key.
#   get key node           Map/List/Options lookup; missing keys yield
#                          the None type literal.
#   get key object         Object instance field access.
#   get i array            Array element by index.
#   get key None           short-circuits to None — None propagation
#                          for chained reads.
# Map/List read patterns also live in map.tsv / list.tsv. This file
# covers the cross-cutting surface: Store-side set/get via `context`,
# Object set, and the None / error paths.
# Runs against the production aql registry.
# tokens
column3: note

column1: # 1. Store via `context`: set key value, then read it back
# `set` on a Store is copy-on-write: each set creates a new Store
# layer and propagates the update up through parent layers to the
# context stack. Re-querying the context returns the latest layer.
# Idiom: `context set KEY VAL end` — `context` pushes the current
# context Store onto the stack, `set` then forward-collects KEY and
# VAL, and `end` halts forward collection so the third arg (the
# container) is taken from the stack.
context set 'x' 42 end context get 'x'
column3: String key — round-trip through the context store

column1: context set ( quote name ) 'Alice' end context get 'name'
column3: Atom key — `(quote name)` forces the atom to materialise before set's forward-collection consumes it

column1: # Multi-key context — every `set` extends the layer chain, every
# `get` walks it.
context set 'a' 1 end context set 'b' 2 end context get 'a'
column3: first key still reachable after second set

column1: context set 'a' 1 end context set 'b' 2 end context get 'b'
column3: second key visible after its own set

column1: # Setting the same key twice — the latest value wins (the new layer
# shadows the old one).
context set 'x' 1 end context set 'x' 2 end context get 'x'
column3: shadowing semantics

column1: # Store value flows into normal expression flow.
context set 'n' 7 end context get 'n' add 3
column3: get-result chains into arithmetic

column1: # Missing key on a Store raises an error.
context get 'missing'
column3: Store get is strict; missing keys are an error

column1: context get quote nope
# 2. Object-instance set
# `set` on an Object instance is in-place mutation: the instance's
# field map is updated directly. The Object value held by `def`
# observes the change on subsequent reads.
# String-key set
type Foo object { a:String b:Integer } def f make Foo { a:'hi' b:1 } set 'a' 'bye' f f .a
column3: String key updates the field

column1: type Foo object { a:String b:Integer } def f make Foo { a:'hi' b:1 } set 'a' 'bye' f f .b
column3: other fields are untouched

column1: # Numeric field via String value — set replaces the value verbatim.
type Foo object { a:Integer } def f make Foo { a:5 } set 'a' 99 f f .a
# 3. None propagation
# `get key None` yields None — lets chained accessor expressions
# short-circuit through missing levels without crashing.
None get 'a'
column3: None passes through string-key access

column1: None get 0
column3: None passes through integer-key access

column1: None get quote x
# 4. Map / List read summary
# Full coverage lives in map.tsv and list.tsv — these rows are a
# quick sanity check that the eng-resident handlers behave the same
# under the production registry.
{ a:1 b:2 } get 'a'
{ a:1 b:2 } get 'b'
{ a:1 } get 'missing'
column3: Map get is forgiving; missing keys yield None
out-of-bounds list access yields None
negative index also yields None
dotted accessor — parser expands to ( map get a )

column1: { outer:{ inner:99 } }.outer.inner
column3: chained dotted access

column1: # 5. Type-literal rejection
# `get` rejects bare type literals with the standard signature_error
# (the matcher refuses non-concrete containers before the handler
# runs).
get 'a' Map
column3: bare Map type literal cannot be indexed

column1: get 0 List
column3: bare List type literal cannot be indexed

column1: # 6. Composition — get inside larger expressions
context set 'n' 5 end ( context get 'n' ) add ( context get 'n' )
column3: parens isolate each context-get; result is 5+5

column1: context set 'n' 7 end def x ( context get 'n' ) x
column3: def captures the looked-up value (paren keeps body coherent)

column1: context set 'msg' 'hi' end def s ( context get 'msg' ) s
